mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which boosts aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery is a key theme for investors.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is negative over any rolling 36-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending. This directly impacts corporate revenues, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) environment, an upside surprise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) increases the probability of an earlier-than-expected monetary tightening, causing a negative repricing of equity risk premia.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% confirms ZIRP regime as of 2012-10-15.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1%, the correlation between 3-month changes in CPIAUCSL and 5-day S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low and falling, it signals a tight labor market that can lead to wage-push inflation. This forces the central bank to tighten policy, which is negative for equities.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive during periods when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and business activity. This is a direct indicator of economic output that translates into higher corporate profits and positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong year-over-year increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals a robust recovery in a key cyclical sector, boosting confidence and spending across the economy, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing has a large multiplier effect on the broader economy.
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of positive returns) for 60-day forward returns following a trigger event is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is above a neutral level and real GDP growth is decelerating, monetary policy is actively restrictive. This combination slows the economy and compresses equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is non-negative during periods when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of a slowing economy. This directly implies lower future aggregate earnings growth for the S&P 500, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity prices are forward-looking claims on economic output.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day returns is positive over any rolling 5-year period.
